title: Hieracium merlasicum
intro: Hieracium merlasicum, also known as hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and a rosette of basal leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium merlasicium include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, hawkweed is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in dried form to make infusions and capsules, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
